articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Portugal thrash Sweden in Euros warm-up for 11th straight win
article.description: Portugal swept past Sweden 5-2 in a friendly on Thursday as manager Roberto Martinez rested Cristiano Ronaldo and several other key players in the build-up to the European Championship in Germany, which starts in June.
article.linkText: Portugal thrash Sweden for 11th straight win

images.name: Portugal celeb vs. Sweden_20240321 [1296x729]
images.alt: Bruno Fernandes of Portugal celebrates with Pepe of Portugal and Rafael Leao of Portugal after scoring his team's third goal during the international friendly match between Portugal and Sweden on March 21, 2024 in Guimaraes, Portugal.

article.story:
Portugal swept past Sweden 5-2 in a friendly on Thursday as manager Roberto Martinez rested Cristiano Ronaldo and several other key players in the build-up to the European Championship in Germany, which starts in June.

The Portuguese stormed into the Euro 2024 finals by winning all 10 of their qualifiers, with Thursday's result making it 11 wins in a row.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, NWSL more (U.S.)

"It was a good and important test for us," Martinez told reporters after the game. "Sweden have very good strikers. We worked on defensive concepts and it was a good experiment.

"There are other concepts that we need to work on and improve in our next game at Slovenia. It was great to play against a talented team of this level."

Martinez's side opened a three-goal lead in the first half thanks to strikes by Rafael Leão, Matheus Nunes and Bruno Fernandes.

Leao opened the scoring in the 24th minute, firing a brilliant strike into the top corner from a rebound after Bernardo Silva burst into the box and unleashed a curling shot off the post.

In the 33rd minute, Manchester City midfielder Nunes sliced through the Swedish defence before darting a low strike just inside the right post to extend the locals' lead at Guimaraes, before Fernandes tapped in a close-range effort from a Nelson Semedo cross in the last play of the half.

Substitute Bruma extended their lead with first-touch effort in the 57th minute, but Viktor Gyökeres hit back for Sweden from close-range a minute later.

Goncalo Ramos struck from inside the box in the 62nd minute to extend Portugal's lead before Sweden's Gustaf Nilsson netted a stoppage time header in the last play of the match.

Portugal will face Slovenia on Tuesday, with Martinez set to bring in Ronaldo among a host of changes.

"The players that will rest in the the next game are Bernardo Silva, Rúben Dias, João Palhinha, Nélson Semedo, Tote Gomes, Rafael Leão, Bruno Fernandes and Goncalo Ramos.

"Hence, eight players will leave and another eight players will arrive at a high level for the second game, with hunger and a lot of energy, I hope."

Information from ESPN's Adriana Garcia and Reuters contributed to this report.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Portugal 1, Sweden 0. Rafael Leão (Portugal) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the top right corner.
Goal!  Portugal 2, Sweden 0. Matheus Nunes (Portugal) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Bernardo Silva.
Goal!  Portugal 3, Sweden 0. Bruno Fernandes (Portugal) right footed shot from very close range to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Nélson Semedo.
Second Half begins Portugal 3, Sweden 0.
Substitution, Portugal. Bruma replaces Rafael Leão.
Substitution, Portugal. Toti Gomes replaces Nuno Mendes.
Substitution, Portugal. António Silva replaces Pepe.
Goal!  Portugal 4, Sweden 0. Bruma (Portugal) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Bruno Fernandes.

Substitution, Sweden. Emil Krafth replaces Emil Holm.
Substitution, Sweden. Emil Forsberg replaces Anthony Elanga.
Goal!  Portugal 5, Sweden 1. Gonçalo Ramos (Portugal) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Nélson Semedo with a cross.
Substitution, Portugal. Jota Silva replaces Gonçalo Ramos.
Substitution, Portugal. João Neves replaces Matheus Nunes.
Substitution, Sweden. Gustaf Nilsson replaces Viktor Gyökeres.
Substitution, Sweden. Samuel Gustafson replaces Mattias Svanberg because of an injury.

Substitution, Portugal. João Mário replaces Nélson Semedo.
